name: HMS Cornelian (T 15)
events: 16 Jan 1941 HMS Unbeaten (Lt. E.A. Woodward, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Dartmouth with HNoMS Mansfield (Cdr. F. Ulstrup, RNorN) and HMS Cornelian (T/Lt. H.F. Hayes, RNVR). (1)

18 Dec 1941 In a signal timed 0001 A/18, HMS P 38 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, DSC, RN) sent the following signal; Capt. S.5 signal timed 2050 A/17. My position is 49°40'N, 06°56'W. course 085°. E.T.A. outer end Q.Z.S. 278 1100 A/18.

In a signal timed 0245 A/18, the Capt. S.5 sent the following to HMS P 38; My 2050 A/17. Complete your passage on the surface at your discretion and make rendezvous with trawler HMS Cornelian (T/A/Lt.Cdr. S. Gorrell, RNR) at 1000 A/18 at the end of Q.Z.S. 278.
At 1045 A/18, HMS P 38 made rendezvous with the escort.
At 1300 A/18, HMS P 38 ended her 2nd war patrol at Plymouth. (2)

26 Feb 1943 HMS P 511 (Lt. C.R. Pelly, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Plymouth with HMS Tynedale (Lt. J.J.S. Yorke, DSC, RN), ORP Krakowiak (Lt.Cdr. W. Maracewicz) and later with HMS Cornelian (T/A/Lt.Cdr. S. Gorrell, RNR). (3)
25 Apr 1943 HMS Taku (Lt. A.J.W. Pitt, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Plymouth.

First with HMS Ulva (Skr. E.R. Harris, RNR) and HMS Cornelian (T/A/Lt.Cdr. S. Gorrell, RNR) and then with ORP Krakowiak (Kpt.mar. (Lt.Cdr.) J.A. Tchorznicki) and HMS Goathland (Lt.Cdr. E.N. Pumphrey, RN, DSO and 2 Bars, DSC, RN). (4)

11 Feb 1945 HMS Cornelian (T/A/Lt.Cdr. S. Gorrell, RNR) picks up 16 survivors from the Belgian merchant Persier that was torpedoed and sunk by German U-boat U-1017 off the Eddystone Lighthouse in position 50°14'N, 04°20'W.
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
